species.name: Pacific Halibut
species.technique: Bottom fishing with bait (salmon heads, herring, octopus) on circle hooks
species.bestBait: Salmon head strips, whole herring, octopus
species.habitat: Sandy/muddy bottom near ledges, drop-offs, and pinnacles
species.bagLimit: 2 per day, 1 must be under 32 inches
species.fightStyle: Powerful head shakes, bulldogs at depth, heavy sustained pressure
species.bestTime: Tide slack, especially incoming tide change

species.name: King Salmon
species.season: May through July (varies by river system)
species.technique: Trolling with downriggers, mooching, back-trolling plugs in rivers
species.bestBait: Whole plug-cut herring, hoochies behind flashers, Kwikfish plugs
species.habitat: Along coastal migration corridors, near bait concentrations, in river runs and holes
species.bagLimit: 1 per day saltwater, annual limits apply; varies by river for freshwater
species.fightStyle: Powerful sustained runs, goes deep and sulks, strong boatside surges
species.bestTime: Early morning, low light conditions, tide change in saltwater

species.name: Silver Salmon
species.season: Late July through October, peak mid-August to September
species.technique: Trolling hoochies, casting spoons and spinners, fly fishing streamers
species.bestBait: Pink/chartreuse hoochies, Pixie spoons, Vibrax spinners, streamer flies
species.habitat: River mouths, along shorelines near estuaries, holding in river runs
species.bagLimit: 3 per day saltwater, 2 per day most rivers
species.fightStyle: Acrobatic leaps, screaming initial runs, directional changes, tail-walks
species.bestTime: Incoming tide at river mouths, early morning in rivers

species.name: Sockeye Salmon
species.season: Late June through August, peak mid-July on the Kenai
species.technique: Flossing with coho flies and long leaders, drift fishing from bank
species.depthRange: N/A (river fishing), fish hold in traveling and resting water
species.bestBait: Coho flies in red/orange, yarn flies, small bare hooks for flossing
species.habitat: River channels, traveling lanes near banks, holding pools
species.bagLimit: 3-6 per day depending on run strength and EO
species.fightStyle: Dogged, powerful runs in current, less acrobatic than silvers, uses current
species.bestTime: Early morning and evening, incoming tide at river mouths

species.name: Yelloweye Rockfish
species.season: Year-round, most caught incidentally during halibut trips
species.technique: Jigging with metal jigs and swimbaits near rocky structure
species.bestBait: Metal butterfly jigs, large swimbaits, herring on dropper loops
species.habitat: Rock walls, pinnacles, and ledges with heavy structure
species.bagLimit: Included in 4 fish aggregate rockfish limit, sub-limits may apply
species.fightStyle: Steady pull, not a strong fighter but difficult to extract from structure
species.bestTime: Any time, often caught while targeting halibut near rocky bottom

species.name: Lingcod
species.season: July 1 through December 31 (closed Jan-Jun for spawning)
species.technique: Jigging large metal jigs and swimbaits along rocky structure
species.bestBait: Large butterfly jigs, 8-inch swimbaits, whole herring
species.habitat: Rocky reefs, ledges, and pinnacles. Ambush predator near structure.
species.bagLimit: 1 per day, 35 inch minimum in most Southcentral waters
species.fightStyle: Violent head shakes, tries to hole up in rocks, powerful surges
species.bestTime: Slack tide when jig presentation is most controlled

species.name: Pink Salmon
species.season: Odd years only (2025, 2027), July through August
species.technique: Casting spoons and spinners, trolling small hoochies
species.bestBait: Small spoons, spinners in pink and orange, small hoochies
species.habitat: Near shore, river mouths, throughout river systems during runs
species.fightStyle: Aggressive but short fights, occasional jumps, good on light tackle
species.bestTime: Morning and evening, incoming tide at river mouths

species.name: Dolly Varden
species.season: Year-round in freshwater, September through November peak on the Kenai
species.technique: Bead fishing under a float, fly fishing with flesh flies and streamers
species.depthRange: N/A (river fishing), behind spawning salmon in shallow runs
species.bestBait: Salmon egg beads (6-10mm), flesh flies, egg patterns, sculpin flies
species.habitat: Behind spawning salmon feeding on eggs, deeper runs and pools
species.bagLimit: Varies by location, generally 5 per day in combined trout/dolly limit
species.fightStyle: Strong runs in current, head shakes, occasional jumps, dogged fighter
species.bestTime: September through November during salmon spawn, egg drift peaks

metadata.region: Alaska — Southcentral, Kenai Peninsula, Cook Inlet, Kachemak Bay
metadata.disclaimer: Bag limits and seasons are approximate. Always verify with ADFG current emergency orders before fishing.
